mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations upward through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. A positive 3-month trend in PAYEMS produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:2012-09-01 PAYEMS at 133500 thousand persons indicates ongoing post-crisis labor recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS trends fail to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25% and remains near the zero lower bound, the Fed signals commitment to extended monetary accommodation. This regime compresses equity risk premiums and supports equity valuations. The mechanism activates only when FEDFUNDS is constrained at the ZLB, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:2012-09-01 FEDFUNDS at 0.14% confirms ZLB regime active
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window in ZLB periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with accelerating housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based demand expansion across consumer and residential investment sectors. This joint signal of demand strength predicts positive equity returns as corporate earnings benefit from increased consumer and construction activity. The interaction effect is non-linear: both series must show positive momentum simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:2012-09-01 RSAFS at 412939 million dollars and 2012-08-01 HOUST at 750 thousand units show demand recovery
failure_condition: Interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint positive signals in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20d returns in > 58% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates when capacity utilization rises and supply-side constraints tighten. A month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO above 0.3% signals robust manufacturing activity and supply-side strength, which supports equity valuations through improved corporate profitability. This mechanism operates over a 5-day horizon as a leading indicator of near-term earnings surprises.
evidence_refs: fact:2012-08-01 INDPRO at 96.8202 index points reflects post-crisis manufacturing recovery
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or INDPRO acceleration > 0.3% fails to produce positive 5d returns in > 52% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2.5% year-over-year signals monetary tightening pressure and erodes real equity valuations. When inflation accelerates, the Fed tightens policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity multiples. This regime-dependent mechanism predicts negative 20-day forward returns when inflation enters the tightening zone, but is dormant in low-inflation regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:2012-08-01 CPIAUCSL at 230.102 index points indicates subdued inflation environment
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-target inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or inflation > 2.5% fails to predict negative 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence. This deterioration predicts negative 60-day forward returns as equity investors anticipate lower corporate earnings from reduced consumer spending and potential recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:2012-09-01 UNRATE at 7.8% reflects elevated unemployment in post-crisis period
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or UNRATE increases > 0.3pp fail to predict negative 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly interacts with housing starts (HOUST) to signal the strength of the demand cycle. When GDPC1 growth accelerates above 2% annualized and HOUST simultaneously rises, the joint signal indicates robust aggregate demand and forward earnings growth. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns when both conditions align.
evidence_refs: fact:2012-04-01 GDPC1 at 13548.5 billion chained 2017 dollars and 2012-08-01 HOUST at 750 thousand units
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint positive signals fail to predict positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline of more than 0.5% month-over-month signals supply-side contraction and potential economic weakness. This sharp contraction predicts negative 5-day forward returns as equity investors reprrice earnings expectations downward in response to manufacturing weakness and forward-looking recession signals.
evidence_refs: fact:2012-08-01 INDPRO at 96.8202 index points provides baseline for measuring sharp contractions
regime_hint: supply-side contraction or early recession signal
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or INDPRO declines > 0.5% fail to predict negative 5d returns in > 55% of triggered instances.
